title: Replace cond with function heads AND a remaining valid cond
prompt:
Given:

```
def classify(record) do
  cond do
    record.type == :customer and record.tier == :gold -> :vip
    record.type == :customer and record.tier == :silver -> :preferred
    record.type == :customer -> :standard
    record.type == :staff -> :staff
    record.created_at && DateTime.diff(DateTime.utc_now(), record.created_at, :day) < 30 -> :new
    true -> :unknown
  end
end
```

The first four cond branches should become function heads. The `:new` branch involves a runtime call (`DateTime.diff/3`), which can't go in a guard, so it must stay in a `cond` inside the catch-all head. Refactor accordingly. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Classifier do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing which branches can be heads and which need cond + runtime call. Tests the judgment boundary.
